headline: Finite-density QCD infrastructure, hot-QCD meson screening, baryon-stopping diagnostics, and analysis-grade physics ML methods were the strongest June 26 themes.
summary: The June 26 scout screened fewer than 50 recent arXiv candidates across nucl-th, nucl-ex, hep-ph, hep-ex, and physics.data-an. The most relevant heavy-ion items emphasize finite-density equation-of-state workflows, hot-QCD meson screening, kinetic assumptions in strongly coupled thermal systems, holographic energy-loss diagnostics, and baryon-stopping observables. The strongest AI/ML items are transferable HEP methodology papers: interpretability/explainability discipline, reinforcement-learning parameter-space search, optimal-transport anomaly representations, and nuisance-aware ML unfolding.
strongest_paper: Studying the QCD Matter produced in Heavy-Ion Collisions using the MUSES Calculation Engine
top_takeaway: The strongest same-day heavy-ion contribution is reproducible finite-density EoS infrastructure, while the most reusable ML contributions target validation language, anomaly representations, and nuisance-aware unfolding.

papers.title: Axial-Vector Lattice Benchmarks Reveal a Common Medium Response of Meson Screening in Hot QCD
papers.authors: Jie Ren; Chen Chen; Fei Gao; Si-xue Qin
papers.abstract: Combines lattice-QCD screening-mass benchmarks with a symmetry-preserving Dyson-Schwinger baseline to model flavor-dependent meson screening in hot QCD.

papers.title: Applicability of kinetic theory in strongly coupled thermal quantum systems
papers.authors: Shile Chen; Shuzhe Shi; Pengfei Zhuang
papers.abstract: Studies finite-temperature momentum distributions and correlation functions in simplified strongly interacting spinor systems to diagnose when kinetic theory becomes applicable.

papers.title: Holographic light-quark energy loss in a spinning plasma
papers.abstract: Computes light-quark stopping distance and energy loss in a spinning holographic plasma, finding anisotropic enhancement of energy loss with the rotation parameter.

papers.title: Studying the QCD Matter produced in Heavy-Ion Collisions using the MUSES Calculation Engine
papers.authors: Johannes Jahan; Kevin P. Pala; Yumu Yang; Isabella Danhoni; Prachi Garella; et al.
papers.abstract: Presents Calliope, the latest MUSES Calculation Engine release for finite-density equation-of-state workflows, thermodynamic derivatives, transport-coefficient interfaces, EoS merging and inversion, and BES-energy hydrodynamic demonstrations.

papers.title: Rapidity-even directed flow splitting of protons and antiprotons as a probe of baryon stopping in relativistic heavy-ion collisions
papers.abstract: Proposes proton-antiproton rapidity-even directed-flow splitting, especially midrapidity curvature, as a discriminator of baryon stopping and diffusion at BES energies.

papers.title: Interpreting "Interpretability" and Explaining "Explainability" in Machine Learning in Physics
papers.authors: Rikab Gambhir; Luisa Lucie-Smith; Jesse Thaler
papers.abstract: Conceptual review distinguishing structural interpretability from domain-grounded explainability and emphasizing task-specific intervention plans.

papers.title: Supercool with PPO: Exploring Supercooled Phase Transitions via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Wan-Zhe Feng; Zong-Huan Ye; Zi-Hui Zhang
papers.abstract: Uses Proximal Policy Optimization to search sparse parameter regions producing detectable gravitational-wave signals from supercooled phase transitions.

papers.title: Optimal Transport Event Representation for Anomaly Detection
papers.authors: Tianji Cai; Aditya Bhargava; Benjamin Nachman
papers.abstract: Introduces optimal transport as a physics-informed event representation for weakly supervised anomaly detection, improving significance on LHC Olympics benchmarks in an idealized setup.

papers.title: Machine Learning-based Unfolding for Cross Section Measurements in the Presence of Nuisance Parameters
papers.authors: Huanbiao Zhu; Krish Desai; Mikael Kuusela; Vinicius Mikuni; Benjamin Nachman; Larry Wasserman
papers.abstract: Extends OmniFold into Profile OmniFold, incorporating nuisance parameters into ML-based unfolding with Gaussian and simulated CMS demonstrations.

papers.title: Kinetic freeze-out and diffusion dynamics in small-system asymmetric collisions at sqrt(sNN)=200 GeV in light of a generalized Fokker-Planck distribution
papers.authors: M. Waqas; Wolfgang Bietenholz; Khusniddin K. Olimov; Muhammad Ajaz; Jihane Ben Slimane; Laila A. Al-Essa; A. Haj Ismail
papers.abstract: Fits small-system neutral-pion spectra with a generalized Fokker-Planck distribution and interprets fitted trends as freeze-out and diffusion dynamics.

papers.title: Thermodynamical analysis of QGP using effective PNJL model with Quasiparticle approach
papers.authors: Yogesh Kumar; Himanshu Aggarwal; R. S. Laishram; Poonam Jain; Om Prakash; et al.
papers.abstract: Extends a two-flavor PNJL model with quasiparticle masses and reports improved agreement with lattice-QCD thermodynamics at zero chemical potential and magnetic field.

papers.title: The Effect of Topological Defects and Magnetic Flux on Fully-Heavy Tetraquarks and Mass Spectra of Heavy Quarkonia Using the Analytical Exact Iteration Method
papers.authors: N. H. Gerish; M. Abu-Shady; E. M. Khokha
papers.abstract: Solves a modified Cornell-potential model with topological-defect and magnetic-flux terms for heavy quarkonia and fully heavy tetraquarks.

papers.title: Magnetic moments of decuplet baryons in isospin asymmetric magnetized strange matter
papers.authors: Hrishika P; Manpreet Kaur; Suneel Dutt; Harleen Dahiya; Arvind Kumar
papers.abstract: Computes in-medium decuplet-baryon masses and magnetic moments in hot, dense, magnetized strange matter using chiral quark models.
